# Supabase Setup — SBTi SME Readiness Assessment

**Date:** 7 June 2026
**Spec version used:** 3.0
**Status:** Confirmed — all steps complete and tested

## Project Details

**Project name:** sustainos
**Supabase project URL:** https://dajnizibuzsvyofiezlg.supabase.co
**Region:** eu-central-1 (Frankfurt)
**Project type:** Existing project — upgraded from v2.0. The v2.0 leads table was dropped and replaced by the submissions table. Supabase Auth was not enabled in v2.0 — it is now enabled and configured.

---

## Tables Created

### submissions
**Purpose:** One row per completed assessment — stores contact details, full assessment state (answers and results as JSONB), and delivery status.

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| id | uuid | Primary key, auto-generated |
| name | text | Required |
| email | text | Required |
| company_name | text | Required |
| assessment_answers | jsonb | Required — all assessment answers captured from client state at form submit |
| assessment_results | jsonb | Required — eligibility outcome, GHG values, target path, target language captured from client state at form submit |
| status | text | Required, default 'pending_verification', CHECK IN ('pending_verification', 'report_sent') |
| created_at | timestamptz | Auto-generated |

**Note:** No user_id field. See Notes for Claude Code.

---

## File Storage

**Active:** No

---

## Row Level Security

**Status:** Enabled on all tables — confirmed

| Table | User type | Can read | Can insert | Can update | Can delete |
|-------|-----------|----------|------------|------------|------------|
| submissions | anon | No | Yes | No | No |
| submissions | authenticated | Own rows only — email match | No | Own rows only — email match | No |

**Policy names confirmed in Supabase:**
- `anon_insert_only` — anon INSERT
- `auth_read_own_by_email` — authenticated SELECT
- `auth_update_own_by_email` — authenticated UPDATE

---

## Authentication

**Auth method:** Magic link (signInWithOtp)
**Signup model:** Open — any user who completes the assessment can receive a magic link. No invite required.
**Roles defined:** N/A — A2 access model, no role differentiation
**First users added:** N/A — open signup, no role assignment required
**Magic link redirect URL:** https://sbti-sme-tool-v3.netlify.app/
**Test login confirmed:** Yes — invite link delivered, redirect to correct URL confirmed

---

## Environment Variables for Netlify

Add all four variables to your Netlify site before Claude Code connects to this database.
Location: Netlify dashboard → Site settings → Environment variables → Add variable.

| Variable name | Where to find it |
|---|---|
| SUPABASE_URL | Project Settings → API → Project URL |
| SUPABASE_ANON_KEY | Project Settings → API → Project API keys → anon / public |
| ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | Your Anthropic account (platform.anthropic.com) |
| RESEND_API_KEY | Your Resend account (resend.com) |

> Never paste any key into your code or any file committed to GitHub.
> The SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are also embedded as JS constants in the HTML file (client-side) — this is intentional and safe as long as RLS is correctly configured, which it is.
> There is no service role key in this tool. The Netlify Function authenticates using the user's own JWT. See Notes for Claude Code.

---

## Notes for Claude Code

**No user_id on submissions table.** The row is inserted by an anonymous user before the magic link fires. There is no Supabase session at insert time, so user_id cannot be set. Do not add a user_id field or reference auth.users on this table.

**Email-based RLS — not the standard auth.uid() pattern.** Authenticated read and update policies use `auth.jwt() ->> 'email' = email` to match the row to the logged-in user. This is the correct pattern for this flow. Do not replace it with auth.uid() = user_id.

**UPDATE policy is for the Netlify Function only.** The `auth_update_own_by_email` policy exists so the /send-report Netlify Function can flip status from 'pending_verification' to 'report_sent' using the user's JWT. No UPDATE code should appear anywhere in the frontend. The frontend inserts once (anon) and never calls UPDATE.

**Status field CHECK constraint.** Only two values are valid: 'pending_verification' (default, set at insert) and 'report_sent' (set by Netlify Function after email is delivered). Any other value will be rejected by the database.

**Netlify Function auth pattern.** After magic link return, the client retrieves the Supabase access_token from supabase.auth.getSession() and passes it to /send-report via POST. The Function initialises a Supabase client using that token — not a service role key — so the authenticated user's RLS policies apply server-side. No service role key is required or used.

**Confirm Resend sender domain is verified before email testing.** The Resend API key alone is not sufficient — the sender domain must be verified in the Resend dashboard before any email will send successfully.
